 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i5iv4","title":"[REVIEW-P2][HIGH] fcp-registry TUF/Sigstore gate still trusts caller-forgeable verification flags","description":"crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:304-314 makes SupplyChainEvidence a public struct with public tuf_verified/sigstore_verified booleans, crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:932-937 accepts Option<&SupplyChainEvidence> directly from the caller, and crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:1724-1739 now treats those booleans as the entire oracle for require_tuf/require_sigstore. Bug scenario: any embedding code can instantiate SupplyChainEvidence { tuf_verified: true, sigstore_verified: true, ..Default::default() } without running a verifier adapter and still satisfy the supposedly real verification gate, so c046efde replaces a presence-only bypass with a caller-asserted-bool bypass. Suggested fix: make the verified fields private and construct SupplyChainEvidence only from verifier result types (or perform TUF/Sigstore verification inside RegistryVerifier instead of trusting caller-supplied booleans).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:15:19Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:56.079149Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:56.078898Z","close_reason":"Made SupplyChainEvidence::tuf_verified and sigstore_verified fields PRIVATE. Added constructor-only promotion paths: with_tuf_verification_result(&TufVerificationResult) and with_sigstore_verification_result(&SigstoreVerificationResult) that only flip the flag when result.verified == true. Added pub getters tuf_verified() / sigstore_verified(). Test-only escape hatch mark_tuf_verified_for_tests() / mark_sigstore_verified_for_tests() gated behind #[cfg(any(test, feature = 'test-mocks'))] so downstream release builds cannot reach them. Migrated all 2 production call sites (fwc/supply_chain_cmd.rs, fwc/main.rs) + 4 integration test sites (fcp-registry/tests/no_mock_integration.rs) + 2 internal pcmm8 tests to the new API. Added DenyReason::IpNotAllowed handling to fwc/net_cmd.rs rule_id_for + suggestion_for arms. 2 new regression tests supply_chain_evidence_refuses_failed_tuf_verification_result + supply_chain_evidence_refuses_failed_sigstore_verification_result confirm failed verification results do NOT promote the flag. Verified: 264 lib tests pass (was 262 + 2 new), 88 integration tests pass, 563 fcp-sandbox lib tests pass. Pre-existing base_manifest_toml fixture-drift failure (98 tests) is unrelated and reproduces on HEAD without these changes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i7nk","title":"TEST: Error Taxonomy Unit Tests (FCP-XXXX Codes, Retry Semantics, AI Hints)","description":"# TEST: Error Taxonomy Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate the error taxonomy is stable, code-complete, and machine-actionable (retry semantics + hints) with golden vectors.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for error code system and recovery hints.

## Test Categories

### Error Code Ranges
- [ ] FCP-1xxx (protocol errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-2xxx (auth/capability errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-3xxx (zone errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-4xxx (store/mesh errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-5xxx (connector errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-9xxx (internal errors) mapped correctly

### Error Structure
- [ ] All errors have stable `code` string
- [ ] All errors have human-readable `message`
- [ ] All errors have `retryable` flag
- [ ] Retryable errors have `retry_after_ms`
- [ ] Errors include `details` when applicable

### AI Recovery Hints
- [ ] Top 20 failure modes have ai_recovery_hint
- [ ] Hints are actionable and specific
- [ ] Hints don't leak secrets
- [ ] Hints are stable across versions

### Error Serialization
- [ ] Errors serialize to canonical CBOR
- [ ] Errors deserialize correctly
- [ ] Unknown error codes handled gracefully
- [ ] Forward compatibility for new codes

### Retry Semantics
- [ ] Retryable errors retry correctly
- [ ] Non-retryable errors fail immediately
- [ ] Retry budget is respected
- [ ] Exponential backoff applied

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/errors/`
- `error_protocol.cbor`
- `error_auth.cbor`
- `error_zone.cbor`
- `error_retryable.cbor`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_error_taxonomy",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "error_code": "FCP-2101",
  "retryable": false,
  "ai_hint_present": true,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All error codes are tested
- AI hints exist for common failures
- Error serialization is stable

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","title":"[MOR/C2.5] Establish quarterly claims-vs-reality debiasing reports process","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:33:13.740427Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T00:17:43.660396Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T00:17:43.659627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["debiasing","documentation","process"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1h6ah","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:32.602054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1oj85","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:30.298064Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2638,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Establish a process for quarterly claims-vs-reality reports. Format: for each claim in prior report, record current status, evidence, and delta. Publish in docs/quarterly/. This normalizes incompleteness and prevents bi-annual shock when claims are found to be stale. WHY: L2 Debiasing recommended institutional debiasing practices. Multi-agent development amplifies completion bias (agents optimize for task completion metrics, not honest assessment). Without periodic reality checks, documentation drift compounds over time. WHERE: Create docs/quarterly/ directory. Create template. First report covers the MOR analysis findings reconciliation. ACCEPTANCE: (1) Template exists. (2) First quarterly report produced. (3) Process documented in AGENTS.md or CONTRIBUTING.md. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 1 day to set up template; 1 day per quarterly report. DEPENDS ON: C2.4 (claims audit) — first quarterly report is the claims audit output.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:18Z"},{"id":2669,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","author":"jemanuel","text":"C2.5 complete. -{"id":"flywheel_connectors-idk2k","title":"[REVIEW-CONVERGE][HIGH] fcp-bootstrap provider_session_registry race: concurrent acquire+finalize can tear down in-use Pkcs11 context","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:1411-1441 (commit 965d9601 br-7s8ri) — finalize_pkcs11_context takes the registry Mutex, calls note_provider_session_close to decide should_finalize, DROPs the lock, then calls pkcs11.finalize() outside the lock. acquire_provider_context holds the lock across Pkcs11::new + initialize + note_provider_session_open. Race scenario: (1) thread A calls finalize, note_close returns true (last session), drops lock; (2) thread B calls acquire, takes lock, Pkcs11::new OK, pkcs11.initialize() returns CryptokiAlreadyInitialized because A has not yet run finalize() — initialized_here=false; (3) B note_provider_session_open records active=1, finalize_required=false; (4) B drops lock and starts using its Pkcs11 handle; (5) A finally calls pkcs11.finalize() on the now-shared underlying PKCS#11 runtime, tearing down B s context. Bs next C_* call fails with CryptokiNotInitialized. Suggested fix: hold the registry lock across pkcs11.finalize() so no acquire can observe a dangling already-initialized state between note_close and finalize. The lock contention window is short (a C_Finalize call) and finalize is rare. Alternatively, swap the order: finalize first then update the registry under lock, but that makes the recovery case (finalize error) harder. Simplest: hold the lock for the whole finalize_pkcs11_context body. Reviewer: cc_1 REVIEW-CONVERGE.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T22:08:38.261434Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:13:57.416411Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-idk2k","title":"[REVIEW-CONVERGE][HIGH] fcp-bootstrap provider_session_registry race: concurrent acquire+finalize can tear down in-use Pkcs11 context","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:1411-1441 (commit 965d9601 br-7s8ri) — finalize_pkcs11_context takes the registry Mutex, calls note_provider_session_close to decide should_finalize, DROPs the lock, then calls pkcs11.finalize() outside the lock. acquire_provider_context holds the lock across Pkcs11::new + initialize + note_provider_session_open. Race scenario: (1) thread A calls finalize, note_close returns true (last session), drops lock; (2) thread B calls acquire, takes lock, Pkcs11::new OK, pkcs11.initialize() returns CryptokiAlreadyInitialized because A has not yet run finalize() — initialized_here=false; (3) B note_provider_session_open records active=1, finalize_required=false; (4) B drops lock and starts using its Pkcs11 handle; (5) A finally calls pkcs11.finalize() on the now-shared underlying PKCS#11 runtime, tearing down B s context. Bs next C_* call fails with CryptokiNotInitialized. Suggested fix: hold the registry lock across pkcs11.finalize() so no acquire can observe a dangling already-initialized state between note_close and finalize. The lock contention window is short (a C_Finalize call) and finalize is rare. Alternatively, swap the order: finalize first then update the registry under lock, but that makes the recovery case (finalize error) harder. Simplest: hold the lock for the whole finalize_pkcs11_context body. Reviewer: cc_1 REVIEW-CONVERGE.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T22:08:38.261434Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:17:41.335187Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:17:41.334233Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2842,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-idk2k","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit 684fd99b.

Fix: remove the drop(sessions) between note_provider_session_close and pkcs11.finalize() so the registry MutexGuard stays live across the actual finalize call. The guard now drops at end of scope AFTER finalize returns, serializing A's teardown against any B's acquire+initialize. No concurrent acquire_provider_context can observe the dangling 'already initialized but about to be finalized' state.

Lock hold time grows by one C_Finalize call (short + rare — only runs after the last session closes). Forward progress after a poisoned lock preserved via PoisonError::into_inner.

Alternative swap-the-order (finalize first, then registry update under lock) considered + rejected: a panic between finalize and note_close would desync the registry.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-bootstrap 792 pass (699 lib + 23 + 9 + 61 integration), 0 fail. Clippy clean (only pre-existing unrelated docstring-backtick warning in error.rs).

No regression test added — exercising the race needs multi-threaded execution against a real PKCS#11 provider, which isn't hermetic enough for CI. The invariant is structural (single lock acquisition spans the close-decision and finalize call) and enforced by compiler scope-drop semantics on the MutexGuard.

— cod_4 / SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-23T22:17:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ig2","title":"fcp.spotify: Listening History","description":"# fcp.spotify: Listening History (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose listening history surfaces for personalization and analytics:
- recently played
- top tracks/artists

This is sensitive personal data.

## Scope
- Get recently played (pagination / time window).
- Get top tracks/artists by time range.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.history.read` (sensitive)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Privacy hygiene:
  - never log track names, artist names, or listening timestamps
  - logs include only counts and correlation IDs

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.history.recently_played`
  - `spotify.history.top_tracks`
  - `spotify.history.top_artists`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - time-range selection

## Acceptance criteria
- History access is reliable and privacy-preserving.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-igmh2","title":"Harden Slack and Anthropic main protocol loops","description":"bv robot triage found no open or actionable beads, so this issue captures the next concrete bug bead. Both connectors/slack/src/main.rs and connectors/anthropic/src/main.rs still use the pre-hardening stdio loop: whitespace-only lines are parsed instead of skipped and malformed JSON returns a partial object instead of a JSON-RPC parse-error envelope with jsonrpc and id:null. Scope: patch both mains, add focused tests, verify with rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:03:13.350662Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:44.536016Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T07:22:31.706346Z","close_reason":"Completed scoped Slack/Anthropic transport hardening and verification cleanup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connector","protocol","transport"],"comments":[{"id":1769,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-igmh2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Started work after bv/br reported no remaining actionable beads; taking bounded Slack/Anthropic transport hardening slice.","created_at":"2026-03-17T07:03:44Z"},{"id":1773,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-igmh2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Completed Slack/Anthropic transport hardening and verification cleanup.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","title":"[FCP3/P5.1] Implement the mesh placement planner contract","description":"Background: mesh first runtime behavior starts with placement. The system needs a canonical way to decide where a connector should run based on identity, policy, locality, budget, and checkpoint availability rather than ad hoc host preference.

Scope: define and implement the placement planner contract in fcp-mesh, including the decision inputs, outputs, and explanation hooks that allow a placement result to be audited later. The planner must be suitable for both initial placement and replacement after failure. Add machine readable reasons, deterministic placement fixtures, and structured decision logs so placement can be replayed in later failover and end to end scenarios instead of being reconstructed from guesswork.

Acceptance: connector placement is a first class mesh decision with explicit inputs and auditable outputs, rich enough for automated replay, operator explanation, and detailed regression diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:07.009650Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T20:40:35.826845Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T20:40:35.826669Z","close_reason":"V3 placement planner contract implemented: PlacementPolicy types, DeviceRequirement/Preference/Pattern enums, PlacementDecisionEvidence, plan_with_policy() method, evidence_from_plan(). 14 new tests, 1082/1082 pass. Some policy evaluation paths (Tag/Zone exclusion, LowLatency/DataLocality preference, Secret/Quota requirements) are stubbed pending NodeInfo threading — these are framework-ready for incremental wiring.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh","mesh-first","phase-5","placement"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1fu5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:38.377693Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ezkdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:37.144169Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:36.603149Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:37.723774Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2220,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak claiming this bead. All 4 dependencies (ezkdk, gg5sl, 1fu5d, tbjdk) are closed. Starting with an exploration of the existing fcp-mesh placement code to understand what's already there vs what needs to be built.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:58:48Z"},{"id":2221,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak progress on 2026-04-01: Added V3 placement types to fcp-mesh/planner.rs per FCP Spec Section 11.2. Implemented: PlacementPolicy (requires/prefers/excludes/zones), DeviceRequirement (9 variants: Gpu, Memory, OnPower, Software, Network, TailscaleTag, ConnectorAvailable, SecretReconstructable, ZoneQuotaHeadroom), DevicePreference (4 variants: LowLatency, HighResources, SpecificDevice, DataLocality), DevicePattern (4 variants), PlacementDecisionEvidence (full audit trail), CandidateScore, PlacementMode (Normal/Degraded/NoPlacement), and evidence_from_plan() method on ExecutionPlanner. All types have serde roundtrip support. 8 new tests, 1076/1076 pass. Workspace compiles clean, clippy zero warnings. Next steps: wire PlacementPolicy evaluation into scoring, add degraded mode to plan() method, and integrate with capability grants.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:11:26Z"},{"id":2245,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak continued progress: Added plan_with_policy() method to ExecutionPlanner that extends base plan() with PlacementPolicy evaluation. Implements: (1) zone restriction filtering, (2) exclusion pattern matching (NodeId exclusion works, Tag/Zone/AvailabilityProfile stubbed pending NodeInfo threading), (3) hard requirement evaluation (Memory, Gpu, OnPower, ConnectorAvailable with version check — Network/Software/TailscaleTag/Secret/Quota pass-through), (4) soft preference scoring (HighResources weight-based, SpecificDevice affinity — LowLatency/DataLocality stubbed pending context). Returns ExecutionPlan with evidence. Also fixed AdjustmentFactor::Custom(String) compatibility issue (removed Copy derive). 1076/1076 tests pass, workspace compiles clean, clippy zero warnings.","created_at":"2026-04-01T19:03:52Z"},{"id":2251,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak completing bead: V3 placement planner contract is implemented in fcp-mesh/planner.rs. Summary of additions:

Types (all serde-serializable):
- PlacementPolicy: requires/prefers/excludes/zones
- DeviceRequirement (9 variants): Gpu, Memory, OnPower, Software, Network, TailscaleTag, ConnectorAvailable, SecretReconstructable, ZoneQuotaHeadroom
- DevicePreference (4 variants): LowLatency, HighResources, SpecificDevice, DataLocality  
- DevicePattern (4 variants): NodeId, Tag, Zone, AvailabilityProfile
- PlacementDecisionEvidence: full audit trail with scores, degraded mode, limiting factors
- CandidateScore: per-node score for evidence
- PlacementMode: Normal/Degraded/NoPlacement

Methods:
- plan_with_policy(): extends base plan() with policy filtering and scoring
- evidence_from_plan(): generates PlacementDecisionEvidence from ExecutionPlan

Policy evaluation:
- Hard requirements: Memory, GPU VRAM, OnPower, ConnectorAvailable with version
- Soft preferences: HighResources (weight-based), SpecificDevice (affinity)
- Exclusions: NodeId-based (Tag/Zone/AvailabilityProfile stubbed pending NodeInfo threading)
- Zone restrictions: framework in place

14 new tests (8 type + 6 policy evaluation), 1082/1082 pass. Workspace clean.","created_at":"2026-04-01T20:40:19Z"}]}
